paper.usage: Historical Bauhaus canvas reference. Current TUI surfaces normally inherit terminal background.

muted.usage: Borders, secondary labels, inactive metadata, timestamps, and dimmed separators.

faint.usage: Inset trace tint and low-emphasis reasoning surfaces.

red.usage: Primary accent, prompt caret, ticker label, destructive choices, and errors.

yellow.usage: Running tools, warning capacity state, and active work indicators.

blue.usage: System messages, primary safety choices, and informational emphasis.

green.usage: Successful tool results and completed work indicators.

display.purpose: Rare ASCII titles and future web UI terminal artifacts.

title.purpose: Frame title bars, section headers, palette titles, and tool names.

body.purpose: Chat, markdown, tool args, previews, and queue entries.

label.purpose: Status pills, mode labels, keyboard hints, and semantic state.

frame.structure: Thick border, title bar with `▓▓`, uppercase title, optional right status.

prompt-row.structure: Divider row, wrapped input row with mode pill and caret, hint/status row.

tool-card.structure: Border-only tool record with name, params, status glyph, elapsed time, metadata, and preview.

markdown-renderer.structure: Markdown rendered as text primitives: markers, rails, bullets, code prefixes, and role styles.

rules: Use semantic color for state, not decoration.
Use glyphs, rails, brackets, and box drawing as interface primitives.
Keep busy layouts secondary to the focused main view.
Avoid neon hacker styling, SaaS admin styling, and toy chatbot affordances.
